name: How I Met Your Mother
description: Ted, Marshall, Lily, Robin, and Barney's decade-long story

questions.q: Who is the main character whose story is narrated throughout 'How I Met Your Mother'?

source.notes: Architect. Played by Josh Radnor. Narrates to his kids in the year 2030.

questions.q: Who is the voice of 'Future Ted,' narrating the show to his kids?

source.notes: Bob Saget narrated the voice-over; Josh Radnor played young Ted on screen.

questions.q: Which actor plays Barney Stinson?

source.notes: Harris won 4 Emmy nominations for the role. Also known for 'Doogie Howser M.D.'

questions.q: Which actress plays Canadian TV reporter Robin Scherbatsky?

source.notes: Smulders went on to play Maria Hill in the Marvel Cinematic Universe.

questions.q: Which actress plays Lily Aldrin?

source.notes: Hannigan also played Willow on 'Buffy the Vampire Slayer' and Michelle in 'American Pie.'

questions.q: What is the name of the bar where the gang regularly meets?

source.notes: Located below Ted, Marshall, and Lily's apartment.

questions.q: What is Barney's catchphrase, often broken up by an interruption?

source.notes: 'Legen-wait for it, and I hope you're not lactose intolerant because the next word is-dary.'

questions.q: What is the name of the woman Ted ultimately marries (the 'Mother')?

source.notes: Played by Cristin Milioti. Revealed in Season 8 finale. Show ended 2014.

questions.q: What instrument does Marshall play that becomes a recurring joke on the show?

source.notes: Actually most closely associated with the mother — she plays bass. Marshall famously plays guitar.

questions.q: Which actor plays Ted's best friend Marshall Eriksen?

source.notes: Segel also co-wrote 'Forgetting Sarah Marshall.' Known for 'Freaks and Geeks.'

questions.q: What is Ted Mosby's profession?

source.notes: Architect first, then teaches architecture at Columbia. Designs the GNB skyscraper.

questions.q: Which Canadian pop star is Robin Scherbatsky's teen alter ego?

source.notes: Hit song: 'Let's Go to the Mall.' Later becomes edgier as 'Robin Daggers.'

questions.q: What is Barney Stinson's signature wardrobe item that he takes very seriously?

source.notes: Catchphrase: 'Suit up!' He wrote 'The Bro Code' and 'The Playbook.'

questions.q: Marshall and Lily's baby son is named what?

source.notes: Marvin Wait-for-it Eriksen. Named after Marshall's father.

questions.q: Where does Ted's story begin — what city is the show set in?

source.notes: Manhattan specifically. Show ran 9 seasons (2005-2014) on CBS.

questions.q: Which character spends time living in Minnesota throughout the series?

source.notes: Marshall is from St. Cloud, Minnesota. Huge Vikings fan.

questions.q: Robin is originally from which Canadian city?

source.notes: Actually Vancouver. Her 'Let's Go to the Mall' music video was filmed at the West Edmonton Mall.

questions.q: Whom does Barney eventually marry (before they divorce at the end of the series)?

source.notes: Season 9 essentially covers their wedding weekend.

questions.q: What is the name of Marshall's company-law job that he hates (before becoming a judge)?

source.notes: GNB — parent company Altrucell is where Marshall is at one point. Barney also works at GNB.

questions.q: Who is the owner of MacLaren's Pub?

source.notes: Carl MacLaren, played by Joe Nieves. The real-life bar inspiration was McGee's Pub in NYC.
